perturbation.original_text: The Parties will maintain complete confidentiality regarding this Agreement and all transactions occurring thereunder, each other's business, business sources and affiliates and each other's propriety knowledge and know-how, and will disclose such information only pursuant to the express written permission of the party who made such information available save where such information deemed to be in the public domain or under the order of a competent Court or Government Agency.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will maintain complete confidentiality regarding this Agreement and all transactions occurring thereunder, each other's business, business sources and affiliates and each other's propriety knowledge and know-how, and may disclose such information pursuant to the express written permission of the party who made such information available save where such information deemed to be in the public domain or under the order of a competent Court or Government Agency.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by changing 'will disclose such information only pursuant to the express written permission' to 'may disclose such information pursuant to the express written permission.' This change significantly weakens the obligation to maintain confidentiality, implying that disclosure is now optional even without explicit permission. This change is a contradiction to section 1d) because the party can disclose information even though, they are only allowed with an agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement and each additional agreement concluded or written or verbal disclosure made between the Parties, shall be kept confidential and is not to be reproduced, communicated or distributed in any manner whatsoever except on a "need to know" basis to persons directly involved with the closing of any transaction contemplated between the Parties, or legal counsel of a Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the parties 'will disclose such information only pursuant to the express written permission'. The changed text modifies this to 'may disclose such information pursuant to the express written permission.' This change weakens the confidentiality obligation, making disclosure optional even with express permission. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that the agreement 'shall be kept confidential and is not to be reproduced, communicated or distributed in any manner whatsoever'. The change from 'will' to 'may' creates a direct contradiction as the contradicted text mandates confidentiality, while the changed text allows for optional disclosure, even when the contradicted text forbids it.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be modified only by written agreement duly executed by all Parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be modified by verbal agreement of all Parties hereto.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the agreement can now be modified by 'verbal agreement of all Parties hereto', directly contradicting the original clause that stipulates modifications can 'only' be made by 'written agreement duly executed by all Parties hereto.' This directly undermines the enforceability and reliability of the agreement by introducing a conflicting method of modification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be modified by verbal agreement of all Parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement can only be modified by written agreement, while the changed text states that the agreement can be modified by verbal agreement. This directly contradicts the original condition of written agreement, leading to an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text states 'This Agreement may be modified by verbal agreement of all Parties hereto' which directly is a contradiction of the original text
